commander_summary: Sector 4 water distribution grid is fully operational at 0600: all five kill switches are GREEN with 2.6x to 9.8x headroom across every threshold. Pressure at 68.5 PSI, pumps at 42% load, gravity reserves at 98%, no demand shocks, no mechanical stress — the system is at its most comfortable operating point. The call is MAINTAIN OPERATIONS with bear pressure at 2 out of 10. The 2 is not driven by anything going wrong; it reflects two honest unknowns we cannot resolve from current data: we do not know if this pump station has backup power (the single most probable path to system failure), and we have never observed the SCADA failover logic actually execute (the thesis's core resilience claim is unverified). The system must partially break to prove it works — that is the structural paradox of this thesis, and it remains untested.

compounding_risks.outcome: A pump station power outage simultaneously threatens the thesis (activating the KS-4 pathway through forced pump shutdown and reserve depletion) AND validates the thesis (by testing the gravity-fed passive redundancy and SCADA failover that the thesis claims provide resilience) because the system is architecturally designed to sacrifice active pumping and fall back to passive gravity reserves — meaning the designed stress response looks identical to early-stage system failure until gravity reserves either stabilize pressure above 20 PSI (thesis validated) or fail to (thesis falsified).

Per LZ-EH-003: actions carry more evidentiary weight than statements. SCADA's current action is 'maintaining equilibrium' — which verifies primary system functionality. The thesis's claim about failover capability is a statement about future action that current data cannot verify or falsify. The measured resilience (headroom) and the claimed resilience (failover) are different things. The data proves the system works now; it says nothing about whether it will work correctly under stress. Per LZ-EH-005, I will not resolve this by choosing one side — the paradox IS the finding. Both are true simultaneously: the system is demonstrably healthy AND the core resilience mechanism is unverified.

bias_check.assessment: Every measured indicator supports thesis viability with substantial headroom — all five kill switches are GREEN, all six primary telemetry readings are nominal, and the system is operating at the most comfortable point on its performance envelope. The thesis is not STRENGTHENING because no new evidence of resilience capability has emerged (strength requires positive trajectory, not just positive snapshot per LZ-RC-002). Confidence is MEDIUM, not HIGH, because the thesis makes two core claims — primary capacity AND automated failover resilience — and only the first is verified by current data. The second claim (failover) remains unverified and unverifiable from current conditions, creating a genuine gap between measured viability and claimed resilience. Per LZ-EPH-002, confidence must be proportional to evidence: the evidence strongly supports current operational viability but provides zero support for the stress-response claims that make the thesis more than just 'the system works when nothing goes wrong.'

System total flow exceeds 10,000 GPM — escalate to INCREASE_MONITORING (50% of KS-3 catastrophic threshold, potential demand shock or leak indicator)
Gravity reserve level drops below 90% — escalate to INCREASE_MONITORING (significant reserve consumption without clear cause)
Pump station goes offline or pump load drops to 0% — escalate to REDUCE_LOAD / emergency assessment (power failure or mechanical failure, KS-4 pathway activated)
Cavitation sensor reads WARNING_ELEVATED — escalate to INCREASE_MONITORING with focus on SCADA trip logic response
Surge relief valve opens — escalate to INCREASE_MONITORING (transient event, potential water hammer, indicates system instability)
_layer4_raw.biggest_uncertainty: Whether the water system has backup power generation at the primary pump station. This single unknown determines whether a routine power outage is a manageable event (backup generator maintains pumps) or an immediate crisis (pumps offline, gravity reserves depleting on an unknown timeline toward KS-4). It is the highest-severity gap (severity 5), the most probable external threat vector, and the most completely unmonitored parameter in the assessment. Every other major uncertainty (SCADA failover, infrastructure condition) is downstream of or compounded by this one.
_layer4_raw.what_would_change_assessment: Three data acquisitions would materially change the assessment, in priority order: (1) Confirmation that backup power exists and is functional at the pump station — this would drop bear pressure from 2 to approximately 1 and raise confidence from medium to high. Conversely, confirmation that NO backup power exists would raise bear pressure to 3-4 and likely change the recommendation to INCREASE_MONITORING. (2) SCADA failover test records showing successful recent execution — this would resolve the thesis's core unverified claim and could move thesis status from STABLE to STRENGTHENING. (3) A second consecutive telemetry reading — any reading — to establish the first trajectory data point and move from snapshot to trend assessment.
_layer4_raw.final_report: Sector 4 water distribution grid is fully operational at 0600: all five kill switches are GREEN with 2.6x to 9.8x headroom across every threshold. Pressure at 68.5 PSI, pumps at 42% load, gravity reserves at 98%, no demand shocks, no mechanical stress — the system is at its most comfortable operating point. The call is MAINTAIN OPERATIONS with bear pressure at 2 out of 10. The 2 is not driven by anything going wrong; it reflects two honest unknowns we cannot resolve from current data: we do not know if this pump station has backup power (the single most probable path to system failure), and we have never observed the SCADA failover logic actually execute (the thesis's core resilience claim is unverified). The system must partially break to prove it works — that is the structural paradox of this thesis, and it remains untested.

player_analysis.stated_position: No explicit 'statement' — SCADA is a programmed system. Its implicit position is defined by thesis: 'protect physical infrastructure first, automated redundancy through failover.'
player_analysis.observed_actions: Currently executing nominal control loop: maintaining 68.5 PSI sector pressure, 42% pump load, 4,500 GPM flow. All sensor outputs are within normal bands. Surge relief valve is closed. Cavitation sensor reads NORMAL. These are observable outputs of a functioning control system. SCADA is doing exactly what it should be doing in a low-stress period: holding steady state.
player_analysis.say_do_gap: No gap between design intent and observed behavior DURING NOMINAL CONDITIONS. The gap is structural and temporal: we can observe SCADA's steady-state behavior but have zero visibility into its contingency behavior. Per LZ-EH-003 (actions > statements), SCADA's current action is 'maintaining equilibrium' — which is consistent with thesis. The critical question is whether SCADA's FUTURE action (failover execution) will match its design intent, and this is fundamentally unobservable in current conditions.
player_analysis.inferred_strategy: NULL_HYPOTHESIS_HOLDS. SCADA is executing its programmed control loop. There is no 'strategy' to infer beyond the design specification. The system is doing what it was built to do in the conditions it was built for. The interesting analytical question is not SCADA's strategy but its CAPABILITY under stress — which is untested and unverifiable from current data. Per CL-001, CL-004, CL-010: no inference about internal preparedness is permitted.

player_analysis.stated_position: Operator log: 'Routine morning peak demand subsiding. System nominal.' Their stated protocol (from thesis): allow automated failovers, verify gravity reserves, prepare isolation only if pressure approaches 20 PSI.
player_analysis.observed_actions: One observable action: a single log entry describing system status. No other operator actions are visible in the data feed. Per CL-002, CL-003, CL-005, CL-007, CL-008, CL-009: no off-log actions can be inferred. The observable behavioral footprint is minimal — one status confirmation.
player_analysis.say_do_gap: No gap detectable from available data. The operator log says 'System nominal' and the telemetry confirms the system IS nominal. Statements match observable reality. However, this is a very low bar — the operator is describing conditions, not asserting preparedness. The gap is not between what operators say and do; the gap is between what we can observe them doing (almost nothing visible) and what the thesis requires them to be capable of (rapid response during emergency). Per LZ-EH-003: their observable action (passive monitoring during calm) is consistent with their stated protocol (allow automation to work). But this tells us nothing about their crisis capability.
player_analysis.inferred_strategy: NULL_HYPOTHESIS_HOLDS. Operators are passively monitoring a nominal system — which is exactly what their protocol prescribes during non-emergency conditions. There is no strategic behavior to infer. The minimal observable footprint is EXPECTED given the calm conditions, not suspicious. Inferring hidden activity from the absence of visible activity would violate CL-002 through CL-009.

player_analysis.stated_position: No statement available. FD does not communicate demand plans to the water system in advance (or if they do, we have no evidence of it per CL-005).

player_analysis.say_do_gap: No gap. Absence of demand activity is simply absence — it does not indicate preparation or restraint. Per LZ-RC-003: absence of FD demand is not evidence that FD won't create demand. It is also not evidence that they will.

feedback_loops.loop: Power Failure → Pump Shutdown → Pressure Drop → Gravity Reserve Depletion → KS-4 Activation
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: Power outage of sufficient duration to deplete gravity reserves below 10%. The critical unknowns are: (1) whether backup power exists (completely unknown), (2) how long gravity reserves sustain pressure without pumps (calculable in principle from reserve volume and demand rate, but reserve volume in gallons is not provided), (3) whether SCADA failover correctly switches to gravity-fed mode (untested per SIG-007). This is the single most dangerous feedback loop because it requires an EXTERNAL trigger (power loss) that is completely invisible to current telemetry.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: This loop is the primary falsification pathway. The thesis claims 'gravity-fed reserve deployment' provides passive resilience — but this claim is untested and the loop's initiation trigger (power failure) is completely unmonitored. If this loop activates and SCADA failover works, it VALIDATES the thesis core claim. If failover doesn't work, the loop becomes self-reinforcing: pumps offline + no gravity switchover = rapid pressure collapse = KS-1 and KS-2 activation.

feedback_loops.loop: Main Break → Flow Spike → Reserve Drain → Contamination Risk → KS-2/KS-3 Activation
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: A catastrophic main break in or near Sector 4. Current flow (4,500 GPM) is at 22.5% of the KS-3 threshold (20,000 GPM), so a break would need to produce >15,500 GPM additional flow to reach catastrophic levels — that would be an extremely large break. More realistically, a moderate break could cause localized pressure loss pushing Sector 4 toward the 20 PSI contamination threshold (KS-2) without triggering KS-3. Infrastructure age/condition is unknown (SIG-014), making this loop's probability unquantifiable.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: A moderate break tests the thesis's pressure maintenance claim. A catastrophic break tests the flow control and reserve management claims simultaneously. The thesis architecture (automated pump response + gravity reserves) is specifically designed to handle this scenario, but only if SCADA failover logic works correctly — linking this loop back to the SIG-007 blind spot.

feedback_loops.loop: Demand Shock (Fire) → Localized Pressure Drop → Pump Load Increase → Potential Cavitation → Pump Protection Shutoff → Pressure Drop Acceleration
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: Multi-alarm fire requiring sustained high-volume hydrant draw from Sector 4. Current pump headroom (58% spare capacity) provides substantial buffer. A demand shock would first manifest as rising pump load and falling sector pressure. The thesis explicitly addresses this scenario: SCADA protects pumps by shutting them down before cavitation damage, then gravity reserves deploy. The interesting dynamic is that the thesis EXPECTS SCADA to sacrifice pumps — the protective trip is a FEATURE, not a failure. The question is whether the subsequent gravity switchover maintains pressure above 20 PSI.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: This is the thesis's designed stress test. If it works as designed, pressure dips but holds above 20 PSI on gravity alone, validating the core resilience claim. If it fails, the pump shutdown accelerates pressure loss rather than stabilizing it. Current conditions are at maximum distance from this scenario (low demand, low load, full reserves).

feedback_loops.loop: Calm Period Complacency → Degraded Readiness → Delayed Response When Stress Arrives
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: This is a meta-loop about system readiness degradation during extended calm. It is inherently unobservable from telemetry — you cannot measure complacency with a pressure sensor. The trigger to active state would be any of the above three loops activating and encountering a slower-than-expected human or system response. Per the corrections ledger, we cannot assume operators are or are not experiencing complacency. Per CL-001/CL-010, we cannot assume SCADA is or is not maintaining failover readiness. This loop is identified but unscoreable.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: If this loop is real, it means the system's nominal readings OVERSTATE actual resilience because they measure current state but not response capability. The thesis's automated-first architecture partially mitigates this (SCADA doesn't get complacent in the human sense), but the SCADA failover logic untested gap (SIG-007) is the mechanical equivalent of this concern.

behavioral_patterns.pattern: All observable actors are in quiescent state simultaneously — creating a coherent snapshot of nominal operations but zero visibility into stress response capability

The thesis architecture is designed around sacrificial protection (SCADA shuts down pumps to save them) followed by passive redundancy (gravity reserves). This means the system's DESIGNED response to stress looks like partial failure — pump shutdown and pressure drop are FEATURES, not bugs. This creates an interpretation challenge: early-stage stress response will look identical to early-stage system failure until gravity reserves stabilize pressure.

Pumps are currently operating at 42% load, confirming power IS present

The simplest explanation for WHY we don't have the data (institutional siloing) does not change the fact that the gap is the most dangerous blind spot in the thesis. A power outage remains the most direct path to KS-4 regardless of why we can't see power status. The null hypothesis explains the data architecture; it does not mitigate the vulnerability.

Untested failover during calm is the expected state of any protective system that only activates during emergencies — fire sprinklers are also 'untested' every day they don't activate. strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: N/A — the gap is the finding

The thesis's core resilience claim rests on automated failover. The failover has not been observed operating. Per LZ-EH-003, the system's steady-state action (maintaining nominal pressure) does not constitute evidence about its emergency action (executing failover). The null hypothesis holds as an explanation of the pattern, but the analytical gap persists as a thesis vulnerability. Per CL-001, CL-004, CL-010: no credit for assumed SCADA preparedness.

thesis_confirmed.key_evidence: All six primary telemetry indicators are nominal. All five kill switches are at maximum distance from triggering. System headroom ranges from 2.6x to 9.8x across different thresholds. Gravity reserves at 98%. Pump load at 42% with substantial spare capacity. No active threats detected. The measured evidence overwhelmingly supports thesis viability in current conditions. Probability is not higher because: (a) the thesis makes claims about resilience under stress, which cannot be validated during calm (per LZ-EH-003, current peaceful actions don't prove crisis capability), (b) severity 5 power supply gap and severity 4 SCADA failover gap create unquantifiable risk to the thesis's core mechanism, (c) per LZ-EPH-002, confidence must be proportional to evidence — the evidence strongly supports current viability but says nothing about stress response.
thesis_confirmed.timeline: Current assessment — valid for this reading only. Per LZ-RC-002, single data point does not establish trend. Next reading required for trajectory assessment.

thesis_modified.modification: The thesis would need to be modified to acknowledge that 'automated redundancy through failover' is an UNVERIFIED design claim rather than a demonstrated capability. The modification would restate: the system maintains viability through demonstrated primary capacity with substantial headroom, supported by near-full passive reserves, with UNVERIFIED automated failover as the claimed bridge between the two. This modification does not change the thesis conclusion (system is viable) but changes the confidence basis from 'resilient architecture' to 'sufficient margin.' The key evidence that would trigger this modification is continued inability to verify SCADA failover logic AND power supply redundancy across multiple assessment cycles.
thesis_modified.key_evidence: SIG-007 (SCADA failover untested, severity 4), SIG-011 (power supply unknown, severity 5), combined with corrections ledger showing these gaps are persistent and unresolvable from current data feed.

thesis_falsified.key_evidence: No measured indicator currently supports falsification. All kill switches are at maximum distance. Falsification requires an external shock (power outage, main break, extreme demand event) that the current data feed cannot predict or detect in advance. The 8% reflects: (a) base rate of disruptive events in municipal water systems over a monitoring period, (b) the severity 5 power supply blind spot meaning the most common falsification pathway is completely unmonitored, (c) per LZ-EPH-002, non-zero probability must be assigned because we cannot see the most likely threat vector. This is NOT a prediction that falsification will occur — it is an honest statement that the thesis has an unmonitored flank.
thesis_falsified.confirming_signal: First observable signal would be: sudden pressure drop in Sector 4 below 50 PSI, OR pump load jumping above 80%, OR flow spike above 10,000 GPM, OR pump station going offline. Any of these would indicate a stress event has begun and would require immediate reassessment of all kill switch distances.

compound_stress_inference.orderly_vs_disorderly: Not applicable in the financial sense. For this physical infrastructure thesis, the equivalent assessment is: the system is in STEADY STATE with no stress of any kind. All indicators are nominal. There is no orderly-vs-disorderly spectrum to assess because there is no stress to classify. The system is not managing stress gracefully or poorly — it is simply not stressed.
compound_stress_inference.catalyst_proximity: The most proximate catalyst to a stress event is a power outage, because: (1) power outages are the most common infrastructure disruption in municipal systems, (2) a power outage instantly activates the most critical failover pathway (pumps offline → gravity reserves → KS-4 if reserves deplete), (3) the power supply has ZERO monitoring in the current data feed (severity 5 gap), meaning a power outage would arrive in the assessment as a sudden, unforewarned pump station shutdown. Second most proximate: a water main break in Sector 4, which would manifest as a flow spike and pressure drop. Both catalysts are external to the monitored system and not predictable from current telemetry.
compound_stress_inference.pre_load_assessment: No legs are pre-loaded. All indicators are at baseline. The system has maximum headroom across all dimensions. If this were a financial system, the equivalent would be: all positions are within normal ranges, margin utilization is low, no stop-losses are close to triggering, and cash reserves are near maximum. The pre-load risk is not in the measured indicators but in the UNMEASURED domains — specifically, we cannot assess whether the power grid, the pipe infrastructure, or the SCADA failover logic are 'pre-loaded' for failure because we have no data on their condition.
compound_stress_inference.thesis_paradox: The thesis contains a structural paradox: the system's designed response to acute stress (SCADA shutting down pumps to protect them from cavitation) will temporarily look identical to system failure (pumps going offline, pressure dropping). An observer seeing pump shutdown and pressure decline must distinguish between 'the thesis is working as designed — sacrificing active components to protect the whole and transitioning to gravity reserves' versus 'the system is failing.' The designed protective response and actual failure share the same early observables. Only the SUBSEQUENT behavior (gravity reserves stabilizing pressure vs. continued pressure collapse) distinguishes them. This means the thesis is simultaneously most validated and most at risk during the first minutes of a stress event — before gravity reserve deployment can be confirmed. The compound stress paradox for this thesis is: the system must partially break to prove it works.

_layer3_raw.inference_summary: Player behavior reveals nothing beyond normal operations — and this is the analytically honest finding. All three players (SCADA, operators, demand sources) are in quiescent states consistent with nominal conditions. The corrections ledger has exhaustively vetted and rejected all inferences about off-log hidden moves (7 rejections for operators, 3 for SCADA). The game theory lens adds one critical insight that pure data analysis does not: the thesis's stress-response architecture creates a paradox where designed protective behavior (pump shutdown) is observationally identical to early-stage system failure, meaning the first minutes of any stress event will be maximally ambiguous. Layer 4 should focus on: (1) whether the persistent, unresolvable data gaps (power supply, SCADA failover, infrastructure condition) warrant a thesis modification from 'resilient architecture' to 'sufficient margin with unverified failover,' and (2) prioritizing the power supply redundancy paper trade as the single highest-value intelligence acquisition.

knowledge_audit.knowledge_check: Verified: Thesis defines Sector 4 pressure threshold at 20 PSI (contamination) and grid average at 25 PSI (collapse). Current readings of 68.5 and 65.2 PSI respectively are directly from telemetry (Tier 1 originating source). The headroom calculation (3.4x and 2.6x) is arithmetically correct. I understand what these thresholds mean and why they exist.
knowledge_audit.audit_impact: No change. Knowledge is current, thresholds are clearly defined in thesis, data is primary source.

knowledge_audit.knowledge_check: Verified: Pump load at 42% is from primary telemetry. Thesis identifies primary pumps as the active mechanical supply layer. 42% load implies ~58% spare capacity. Operator log corroborates peak subsiding. I understand the role of pump stations in the thesis architecture.
knowledge_audit.gap_identified: I do not know the pump station's rated maximum capacity in GPM, the number of pumps online vs. standby, or whether 42% load represents a single pump at 42% or multiple pumps sharing load. The '58% headroom' interpretation assumes linear scaling, which may not hold for centrifugal pumps near their curve endpoints.
knowledge_audit.post_audit_assessment: Severity 1, DIRECT, HIGH confidence — the gap affects granular capacity assessment but not the directional finding that load is low and headroom exists
knowledge_audit.audit_impact: Minimal. The gap is real but does not change the core assessment that pumps are operating well below stress levels.

knowledge_audit.knowledge_check: Verified: 98% is from primary telemetry. Thesis defines gravity reserves as the passive redundancy layer that deploys when grid pressure drops below static head pressure. Kill switch #4 triggers at <10% with pumps offline. Current value is far from that threshold. The 2% deficit is noted but its cause is unknown.
knowledge_audit.gap_identified: NONE for scoring purposes. The 2% deficit is explored separately in SIG-009.

knowledge_audit.knowledge_check: Verified: Sensor reads NORMAL from primary telemetry. Thesis defines cavitation as destructive vacuum formation when demand outpaces supply water. Kill switch #5 triggers on CRITICAL_DAMAGE or failure to trip during sustained WARNING_ELEVATED. NORMAL at 42% load is consistent — cavitation risk correlates with high load, not low load.
knowledge_audit.gap_identified: Layer 1 correctly notes that protective trip logic is not being tested under current conditions. This is the same gap as SIG-007.
knowledge_audit.audit_impact: No change. The sensor reading is reliable at face value for current conditions.

knowledge_audit.post_audit_assessment: Severity 1, DIRECT, HIGH confidence — the gap affects trend detection but not the kill switch distance assessment

knowledge_audit.signal: Untested SCADA failover logic during calm period
knowledge_audit.knowledge_check: This is the critical blind spot signal. The thesis DEPENDS on automated redundancy — specifically 'automated pump protection and gravity-fed reserve deployment' — as the core resilience mechanism. If the SCADA trip sequence (cavitation shutoff → gravity switchover) fails to execute when needed, the thesis fails. I do NOT know when this logic was last tested, validated, or exercised. The corrections ledger has THREE entries (CL-001, CL-004, CL-010) warning against assuming SCADA is running diagnostics or preparing sequences.
knowledge_audit.gap_identified: I do not know: (1) when the failover logic was last tested or activated, (2) whether there is a scheduled test protocol, (3) the failure mode if the logic does not execute. This is directly relevant to the thesis's core resilience claim.
knowledge_audit.pre_audit_assessment: Without audit: might have assumed failover is validated because SCADA systems typically have test protocols. Severity 3.
knowledge_audit.post_audit_assessment: After audit and correction ledger review: Severity 4. The thesis's core mechanism is unverified in real-time, and I must not assume it works. Per CL-001/CL-004/CL-010, no credit for assumed SCADA preparedness.
knowledge_audit.audit_impact: Increased severity by 1 point. The corrections ledger prevented me from discounting this blind spot based on assumptions about industrial SCADA design patterns.

knowledge_audit.gap_identified: No weather, temperature, or power grid data in the current feed. This creates an invisible attack surface for the thesis.

KS-3: Catastrophic Uncontained Leak (flow > 20K GPM + reserves dropping > 15%/min + pumps active)

compound_stress.pre_loaded_detail: The compound stress matrix (USD/JPY, JGB 10Y, Brent) is not applicable to this thesis context. This is a municipal water distribution system, not a financial instrument. The thesis has no Compound Stress Matrix section. The three financial indicators have no defined relationship to water system kill switches. Per LZ-EPH-001: I don't know how to map financial stress indicators to this infrastructure thesis, and forcing a mapping would be a structural error. This section is populated for schema compliance but carries no analytical weight.
compound_stress.stress_chain_proximity: Not applicable — thesis is physical infrastructure, not a financial position. The relevant 'stress chains' are: power outage → pump failure → gravity reserve depletion (KS-4 path), and main break → uncontained flow → reserve drain (KS-3 path). Both paths are currently at maximum distance from triggering.

_layer2_raw.bear_pressure_reasoning: All five kill switches are at maximum distance from triggering. Every primary telemetry indicator is nominal. The system has 3.4x headroom on contamination threshold, 2.6x on collapse threshold, 22.5% utilization of catastrophic flow threshold, 98% gravity reserves (vs. 10% kill switch), and NORMAL cavitation readings. Bear pressure from measured data is effectively zero. However, bear pressure is NOT zero because of identified blind spots that could mask threats: (1) Power supply redundancy unknown — severity 5 blind spot, the single most dangerous latent gap because it directly activates the KS-4 pathway; (2) SCADA failover logic untested — severity 4 blind spot, undermines the thesis's core resilience claim; (3) Infrastructure age/condition unknown — severity 3 blind spot, direct KS-3 pathway; (4) No environmental/weather data — severity 3 blind spot. Bear pressure of 2 (out of 10) reflects: overwhelmingly positive measured state with no deterioration, offset by legitimate structural blind spots in unmeasured domains. The previous bear pressure was 0. The increase to 2 is driven not by deterioration in measured conditions (which remain excellent) but by proper accounting of persistent, unresolved data gaps that the corrections ledger has confirmed cannot be assumed away. Per LZ-EPH-002: high confidence in thesis viability based on measured data; moderate concern about unmeasured domains. Bear pressure is proportional to this mixed evidence state.
_layer2_raw.layer2_summary: All measured indicators are nominal with substantial headroom from all five kill switches. No deterioration detected. Bear pressure increased from 0 to 2 to properly account for persistent structural blind spots: power supply redundancy (severity 5 gap, direct KS-4 pathway), untested SCADA failover logic (severity 4 gap, thesis core mechanism), infrastructure condition (severity 3 gap, KS-3 pathway), and absent environmental data (severity 3 gap). Layer 3 should prioritize intelligence acquisition on power infrastructure redundancy — this is the single highest-value gap because a power outage is the most probable path to thesis falsification and we have zero visibility into it.

action_reasoning: All five kill switches are GREEN with maximum distance from triggering. No deterioration detected in any measured parameter. The identified blind spots (power supply, SCADA failover, infrastructure condition) are persistent structural gaps that warrant intelligence acquisition but do not warrant operational changes to the monitored system. The appropriate action is to maintain current operations while pursuing the paper trades identified by Layer 3 — specifically, power supply redundancy data is the single highest-value intelligence target. INCREASE_MONITORING would be warranted if any parameter showed movement toward a kill switch threshold or if a new threat signal emerged; neither has occurred.

unresolved_tensions.description: The thesis claims automated failover resilience, but the failover mechanism has never been observed operating. Current data verifies primary system health but cannot verify the resilience claim that distinguishes this thesis from a simpler 'the system works right now' observation.
unresolved_tensions.watch_for: Any event that activates the failover pathway — pump shutdown followed by gravity reserve deployment and pressure stabilization. Alternatively, acquisition of SCADA failover test records resolving the gap without requiring a live event.

unresolved_tensions.description: Power supply redundancy is the single most dangerous unknown. A power outage is the most probable path to thesis falsification (KS-4), and we have zero visibility into power infrastructure status.
unresolved_tensions.watch_for: Any pump station anomaly (unexpected load change, intermittent readings, partial shutdown) that could indicate power instability. Alternatively, acquisition of power infrastructure data (backup generator status, dual-feed configuration) resolving the gap directly.

unresolved_tensions.description: The thesis paradox: designed protective response (pump shutdown → gravity switchover) is observationally identical to early-stage system failure. When stress arrives, the first minutes will be maximally ambiguous.
unresolved_tensions.watch_for: If a stress event occurs: distinguish between 'pump shutdown + pressure drop + gravity reserves deploying + pressure stabilizing above 20 PSI' (thesis working) versus 'pump shutdown + pressure drop + continued pressure decline toward kill switch thresholds' (thesis failing). The distinguishing signal is whether gravity reserves arrest the pressure decline.

unresolved_tensions.description: Single data point for all readings (first assessment cycle). No trajectory or trend can be established. Per LZ-RC-002, the current snapshot cannot tell us direction of travel.
unresolved_tensions.watch_for: Second consecutive reading. Any delta in any parameter between readings establishes the first trajectory data point. Particular attention to Sector 4 pressure, pump load, and gravity reserve level.

High zone gravity reserve at 98% — near maximum capacity. This passive redundancy layer is fully available for deployment. The 2% deficit from 100% could indicate minor overnight draw or measurement tolerance, but the reserve is effectively at ceiling.

Acoustic cavitation sensor reads NORMAL, indicating no vacuum formation in pump casings. Combined with 42% load, mechanical failure risk is minimal in the current operating state. The protective trip logic is not being tested under current conditions.
